channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Escape From Tarkov Icebreaker Update and New Story Chapter
item.content:encoded:
Escape From Tarkov has released a major new update and event for players to experience in the extraction shooter. While details are relatively scarce in Battlestate Games typical way, we'll run down what we do know here.

Icebreaker is a new map that players will be able to explore, and is premiering as part of the Icebreaker event. This is a PvE focused gameplay area and will have you and up to two friends go into the area available in all game modes. There are no random players in your matching here, and you'll only be facing AI enemies. During the chapter you'll be exploring and uncovering how the Paradigm Shipping Icebreaker ended up trapped inside Tarkov's blockade ring. The area includes a new boss you'll be able to battle as well.

To enter the area, players are reporting that you need to get and use the radio in your hideout with a sufficient intel level. An alternative method is finding posters on the second floor of the Terra group building and other areas in the Ground Zero map.

As part of the release, Battlestate games released a new code for freebie items. The code to redeem is: BACKTORAID.

Other Escape From Tarkov Updates

There is a bit more to this update then 'just' the Icebreaker map, event, and Boreas story chapter. The biggest addition is a new mechanic that helps players locating in-raid items needed for tasks with an expanded directional indicator when nearing where the item spawns. While perhaps a step away from the simulationism that Tarkov is known for, players may enjoy not haivng to pixel hunt quite so much.

Several streamer items were also added and the chance of death after injecting Obdolbos 1 was increased to 33%, meaning you really shouldn't drug up kids.

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don
